steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes: "reducing flow velocity and thereby ______ sedimentary particle suspension." The result of the action is stated next: "Particles are thus deposited." The blank must describe what happens to particle suspension — it gets stopped or broken off by the reduced velocity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'compounding': Compounding means increasing or intensifying. If suspension were compounded, particles would stay suspended and not deposit — the opposite of what the passage says. ✗
  • Choice B — 'obscuring': Obscuring means hiding or concealing. Velocity doesn't hide suspension; this word doesn't fit the physical cause-effect being described. ✗
  • Choice C — 'interrupting': Interrupting means breaking off or stopping a process. Reduced velocity interrupts (stops) particle suspension, causing the particles to fall and deposit. This matches the logic precisely. ✓
  • Choice D — 'expediting': Expediting means speeding up. That would increase suspension, not end it, contradicting the depositing of particles. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
